note: raw name is non-ASCII (año) — canonical `year` is worth the rename on its own
survey month of the interview
join key 1/3 (conglome+vivienda+hogar identifies a household)
join key 2/3
join key 3/3
INEI ubigeo (NOT the ONPE ubigeo — never merge across the two)
INEI geographic domain (costa/sierra/selva x urban/rural + Lima)
1-8, population-size stratum; present and stable in every year 2004-2025

1=urban 2=rural. CONFIRMED empirically, not assumed: the rule reproduces INEI's published 2025 poverty — urban 23.41 vs 23.4 official (+0.01pp), rural 35.45 vs 35.5 (-0.05pp). Urban pop share 81.3%, matches known urbanization.

persons in the WELFARE universe = HABITUAL RESIDENTS (module 02 p204==1). CONFIRMED: counting roster rows with p204==1 reproduces mieperho in 100.00% of households and the weighted population to the person (34,856,707, diff +0). It is NOT a domestic-worker/pensionista exclusion — those are 54 rows in 2025. The de facto roster (all rows) gives 37.13M, a 6.5% overcount. See crosswalk enaho_02 -> hh_member.
total household members including those outside the poverty universe
income earners in the household
household expansion factor. Using this alone for a PERSON rate is the classic error: it yields 20.13% poverty vs the official 25.67%.
the official INEI person weight. This is what reproduces the published poverty series to 0.0pp.
1=extreme poor, 2=poor (non-extreme), 3=non-poor. Coding stable in every year. SHIPPED AS-IS — never recomputed.
headcount indicator used by validate.poverty()
extreme headcount indicator
NET annual household income (imputed rent included, INEI convention). Nominal — deflate only via the opt-in.
GROSS annual household income. CONFIRMED: inghog1d >= inghog2d in 100.0% of households in 2004/2014/2020/2025.
NET annual household spending. This is the welfare aggregate INEI ranks against the poverty line.
GROSS annual household spending
MONTHLY PER-CAPITA total poverty line. CONFIRMED by reconstruction: gashog2d/(12*mieperho) < linea reproduces INEI's own pobreza flag in 100.00% of 2025 households (99.78% 2020, 99.18% 2004).
MONTHLY PER-CAPITA extreme (food) line. linpe < linea in 100% of households in every year tested.
the welfare metric that is compared against poverty_line. Nominal.
per-capita monthly income, nominal
spatial price deflator. ABSENT IN 2004 — resolves to an all-NA column, marked in the coverage report, never silently dropped. Needed by the deflation opt-in.
